Scenario: Verify tasks page loads successfully after login
Precondition: User is logged in with valid credentials
Steps: 1. Login with valid credentials (rajeev@yopmail.com / Cirakas@123456)\n2. Navigate to tasks page
Expected Result: Tasks page loads with task list visible

Scenario: Verify user can create a new task
Precondition: User is logged in and on tasks page
Steps:
1. Click on Create Task button
2. Enter task title
3. Enter task description
4. Select priority
5. Select project site
6. Click Save/Submit
Expected Result: New task is created successfully and appears in task list

Scenario: Verify created task is visible in task list
Precondition: A task has been created successfully
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Look for the created task in task list
Expected Result: The created task is visible in the task list with title and description

Scenario: Verify user can edit an existing task
Precondition: A task exists in the task list
Steps:
1. Click on edit/pencil icon on an existing task
2. Modify title
3. Modify description
4. Click Save
Expected Result: Task details are updated successfully

Scenario: Verify task listing displays all tasks
Precondition: User is on tasks page
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Observe the task listing
Expected Result: All tasks are listed with title, description, priority, status, and overdue days (if applicable)

Scenario: Verify reassign button is present for admin user
Precondition: Logged in as admin user and on tasks page
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Open any task card\n3. Check for reassign button
Expected Result: Reassign button is visible on the task card

Scenario: Verify reassign button is present for super admin user
Precondition: Logged in as super admin user and on tasks page
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Open any task card\n3. Check for reassign button
Expected Result: Reassign button is visible on the task card

Scenario: Verify task can be assigned to a single member
Precondition: User has create/edit permissions
Steps: 1. Create or edit a task\n2. In assignee field select a single member\n3. Save
Expected Result: Task is assigned to the selected single member

Scenario: Verify task can be assigned to multiple members
Precondition: User has create/edit permissions
Steps: 1. Create or edit a task\n2. In assignee field select multiple members\n3. Save
Expected Result: Task is assigned to all selected multiple members

Scenario: Verify alert button is present on task card for uncompleted tasks
Precondition: Task is created by user and assigned to another user but not completed
Steps:
1. Login as the task creator
2. Navigate to tasks page
3. Find the task assigned to another user that is not completed
4. Check for alert button
Expected Result: Alert button is visible on the task card

Scenario: Verify calendar view is available on tasks page
Precondition: User is on tasks page
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Click on calendar view toggle
Expected Result: Page switches to calendar view showing tasks on dates

Scenario: Verify list view is available on tasks page
Precondition: User is on tasks page
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Click on list view toggle
Expected Result: Page switches to list view showing tasks in tabular/list format

Scenario: Verify card view is available on tasks page
Precondition: User is on tasks page
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Click on card view toggle
Expected Result: Page switches to card view showing tasks as cards

Scenario: Verify assigned user can start a task
Precondition: Task is assigned to the logged-in user and is in Not Started status
Steps:
1. Login as the assigned user
2. Navigate to tasks page
3. Find the assigned task
4. Click Start button
Expected Result: Task status changes to In Progress

Scenario: Verify assigned user can provide progress on a task
Precondition: Task is in In Progress status
Steps:
1. Login as the assigned user
2. Navigate to tasks page
3. Find the in-progress task
4. Update progress percentage
Expected Result: Progress is updated successfully

Scenario: Verify assigned user can pause a task
Precondition: Task is in In Progress status
Steps:
1. Login as the assigned user
2. Navigate to tasks page
3. Find the in-progress task
4. Click Pause button
Expected Result: Task status changes to Paused

Scenario: Verify pause reason/related task can be specified when pausing
Precondition: Task is in In Progress status and user clicks Pause
Steps: 1. Click Pause on an in-progress task\n2. In the dialog select related task as pause reason\n3. Confirm pause
Expected Result: Task is paused with the related task noted

Scenario: Verify resume button is enabled only when related task is completed
Precondition: Task is paused with a related task dependency
Steps:
1. Navigate to paused task
2. Check resume button state before related task completion
3. Complete the related task
4. Check resume button state again
Expected Result: Resume button is disabled until related task is completed; enabled after related task completion

Scenario: Verify assigned user can resume a paused task
Precondition: Related task is completed and resume button is enabled
Steps: 1. Click Resume button on paused task
Expected Result: Task status changes back to In Progress

Scenario: Verify assigned user can complete a task
Precondition: Task is in In Progress status
Steps:
1. Login as the assigned user
2. Navigate to tasks page
3. Find the in-progress task
4. Click Complete button
Expected Result: Task status changes to Completed

Scenario: Verify tasks can be filtered by status
Precondition: Multiple tasks exist with different statuses
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Select a status filter (e.g. In Progress)
Expected Result: Tasks list is filtered to show only tasks with the selected status

Scenario: Verify tasks can be filtered by priority
Precondition: Multiple tasks exist with different priorities
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Select a priority filter (e.g. High)
Expected Result: Tasks list is filtered to show only tasks with the selected priority

Scenario: Verify tasks can be filtered by project site
Precondition: Multiple tasks exist across different project sites
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Select a project site filter
Expected Result: Tasks list is filtered to show only tasks belonging to the selected project site

Scenario: Verify task card displays title
Precondition: A task exists in the task list
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. View any task card
Expected Result: Task title is displayed on the task card

Scenario: Verify task card displays description
Precondition: A task exists in the task list
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. View any task card
Expected Result: Task description is displayed on the task card

Scenario: Verify task card displays priority
Precondition: A task exists in the task list
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. View any task card
Expected Result: Task priority (High/Medium/Low) is displayed on the task card

Scenario: Verify task card displays status
Precondition: A task exists in the task list
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. View any task card
Expected Result: Task status (Not Started/In Progress/Paused/Completed) is displayed on the task card

Scenario: Verify overdue days are shown on task card for overdue tasks
Precondition: An overdue task exists in the task list
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. View an overdue task card
Expected Result: Overdue days count is displayed on the task card

Scenario: Verify super admin can see all tasks across all project sites
Precondition: Logged in as super admin
Steps: 1. Login as super admin\n2. Navigate to tasks page
Expected Result: All tasks from all project sites are visible

Scenario: Verify admin can see tasks of assigned project sites only
Precondition: Logged in as admin with specific project site assignments
Steps: 1. Login as admin\n2. Navigate to tasks page
Expected Result: Only tasks belonging to the admin assigned project sites are visible

Scenario: Verify project manager can see created and assigned tasks
Precondition: Logged in as project manager
Steps: 1. Login as project manager\n2. Navigate to tasks page
Expected Result: Only tasks created by the PM or assigned to the PM are visible

Scenario: Verify team member can see created and assigned tasks
Precondition: Logged in as team member
Steps: 1. Login as team member\n2. Navigate to tasks page
Expected Result: Only tasks created by the team member or assigned to them are visible

Scenario: Negative: Verify create task with empty title shows validation
Precondition: User is on create task form
Steps:
1. Click Create Task
2. Leave title empty
3. Enter description
4. Click Save
Expected Result: Validation error is shown for empty title; task is not created

Scenario: Negative: Verify create task with empty required fields
Precondition: User is on create task form
Steps: 1. Click Create Task\n2. Leave all required fields empty\n3. Click Save
Expected Result: Validation errors are shown for all required fields; task is not created

Scenario: Edge: Verify task creation with very long title
Precondition: User is on create task form
Steps: 1. Enter a title with maximum allowed characters (e.g. 255 chars)\n2. Fill other fields\n3. Click Save
Expected Result: Task is created successfully with the long title (or truncated gracefully)

Scenario: Edge: Verify filtering with no matching results
Precondition: No tasks exist matching the selected filter
Steps: 1. Select a filter combination that yields no matching tasks
Expected Result: Empty state message is displayed indicating no tasks found

Scenario: Accessibility: Verify tasks page has proper heading structure
Precondition: User is on tasks page
Steps: 1. Navigate to tasks page\n2. Check for proper heading structure (h1, h2 elements)
Expected Result: Tasks page has clear and descriptive headings for navigation

Scenario: Regression: Verify all task operations work after page refresh
Precondition: Tasks were created and modified
Steps: 1. Perform create/edit/assign operations on tasks\n2. Refresh the page\n3. Verify all changes persist
Expected Result: All task operations are persisted after page refresh
